overall.description: Cinematic game engine footage of a grand gothic dining hall reminiscent of Hogwarts Great Hall, featuring thousands of floating candles, long dining tables with seated students, and towering stained glass windows. The camera remains stationary throughout, capturing the warm ambient lighting and medieval architecture.

scene_state.functional_zone: gothic great hall with floating candle chandeliers

agents.ego_description: standing stationary at the hall entrance, observing the dining area and floating candles

notable_agents.action: translucent ghostly figure floating near the foreground, slowly bobbing up and down

entered.detail: hundreds of lit candles floating magically in the air throughout the hall ceiling
large ornate stained glass window at the far end of the hall with blue and gold patterns
long wooden dining tables lined with seated figures and lit candelabras

tall stone columns and arches along the left wall supporting the ceiling
tall stone columns and arches along the right wall supporting the ceiling

segments.scene_description: A static cinematic view of a grand gothic dining hall illuminated by thousands of floating candles. Long dining tables fill the space on both sides with seated figures, while a large stained glass window dominates the far wall. A translucent ghostly figure floats in the right foreground, and the warm candlelight creates a magical atmosphere throughout the stone interior.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot presents a static, cinematic gaze into a grand gothic dining hall where magical illumination defines a timeless atmosphere. It begins with a wide view of the stone interior, where thousands of floating candles cast a warm, flickering glow over long dining tables filled with seated figures. As the eye settles into the space, the composition emphasizes the contrast between the intimate candlelight and the towering stained glass window that dominates the far wall. The scene is further layered by a translucent ghostly figure drifting through the right foreground, adding a spectral presence to the medieval architecture.
